fellowships.description: Early-career fellowship introducing participants to AI governance research and policy practice internationally.
fellowships.notes: Early-career; international eligibility

fellowships.description: Twelve-month funded fellowship supporting technologists and researchers building public-interest tech projects (trustworthy AI, privacy, open infrastructure).
fellowships.notes: Nomination-based (self-nominations welcome), funded, up to 10 fellows globally; trustworthy-AI / public-interest tech. 2026 cycle CLOSED (nominations ended Jan 30, 2026; appointments announced June 2026). Next nominations open early 2027 — watch the page. https://www.mozillafoundation.org/en/what-we-do/grantmaking/fellowship/

fellowships.description: Grants supporting early-career post-PhD scholars from underrepresented backgrounds.
fellowships.notes: Post-PhD early-career scholars only

fellowships.description: Multidisciplinary fellowship funding postdoctoral and pre-tenure researchers tackling hard AI problems.
fellowships.notes: For postdoctoral and pre-tenure researchers; multidisciplinary AI; up to $5.5M cohort funding; post-defense target

fellowships.description: Research grants funding faculty-led education-research projects of varying scale.
fellowships.notes: Faculty PI required; post-PhD horizon target

fellowships.description: Grant funding university-research-practice partnerships that reduce inequality in youth outcomes.
fellowships.notes: Institutional applicant; faculty PI required

fellowships.fellowship: John Robert Lewis Scholars & Fellows Program
fellowships.description: Leadership fellowship developing emerging civic leaders through trips, education, and an oral-history project.
fellowships.terms: US citizen/PR; 18+; current or recent (<24mo) student; full participation required
fellowships.notes: The John Robert Lewis Scholars & Fellows Program. Applications for Cohort 7 open September 2026. Program runs July 2026–July 2027 with a $2,000 stipend. Eligibility: US citizen or permanent resident; 18+; current undergraduate/graduate/seminary student or within 24 months of graduating; exceptional leadership potential; must participate in all trips, educational requirements, and the oral-history project. https://faithandpolitics.org/apply-for-fellowship/

fellowships.description: Funds humanities and social-science doctoral candidates pursuing methodologically innovative dissertation research.
fellowships.notes: Humanistic/interpretive focus did not match data-and-metrics methodology

fellowships.description: One-year placement embedding PhD scientists and engineers in California state policymaking.
fellowships.terms: PhD (or MS in engineering + 3 yrs exp); 1-yr CA Legislature placement; $45K + benefits
fellowships.notes: Bridges data/research and legislative decision-making; parallels Texas Senate analyst + TF-IDF work. Source: CCST via Caltech FASA

fellowships.description: Year-long Harvard fellowship for doctoral and postdoctoral scholars doing policy-relevant research on the governance of AI, biotech, quantum, and other emerging technologies.
fellowships.terms: Doctoral dissertation-writing stage (passed generals); recent PhDs
fellowships.notes: Replaces defunct TAPP. Predoc stipend up to ~$70K; in-residence at Harvard; emerging-tech governance incl. AI. Eligibility: doctoral candidates in dissertation-writing stage. 2027-28 cycle: opens Oct 1, closes Dec 1, 2026; rec letters Dec 15. https://www.belfercenter.org/fellowship/technology-and-geopolitics

fellowships.description: One-year residential Harvard fellowship for doctoral candidates pursuing policy-relevant security research, including technology and AI-security topics.
fellowships.terms: Doctoral program; passed general exams; significant dissertation progress
fellowships.notes: Predoc stipend ~$46K (postdoc ~$56K), one academic year in residence at Harvard; tech/AI-security research welcome. Opens Oct 1, closes Dec 1; non-deferrable, full-time residence required. https://www.belfercenter.org/fellowship/international-security-program-fellowship

fellowships.description: Biennial seminar convening doctoral students in third-sector and nonprofit research internationally.
fellowships.notes: Biennial; international option

fellowships.description: Grants funding applied, openly-published research on Internet governance, access, trust, and the societal impacts of emerging technologies including AI.
fellowships.notes: $200K-$500K per project; open globally to individuals and institutions; applied + openly-published research informing policy. 2026 window closed (Apr 7-May 22); watch for 2027 cycle ~April. Submit via Fluxx (register 24h early). https://www.isocfoundation.org/grant-programme/research-grant-programme/

fellowships.organization: Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society
fellowships.description: Year-long affiliate appointment for scholars and practitioners researching internet, technology, and society.
fellowships.notes: Year-long affiliate appointment; open to scholars, practitioners, policy professionals; multiple cohorts

fellowships.description: Summer associate program giving graduate students hands-on RAND public-policy research experience.

fellowships.fellowship: Doctoral Fellowship in Law and Social Science
fellowships.description: Year-long residential fellowship funding sociolegal research at the intersection of law and social science.
fellowships.notes: $30,000 + benefits. Year-long sociolegal research fellowship; has supported AI/tech policy work; strong methodological credential. Uchicago Only

fellowships.organization: American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS)
fellowships.description: Places PhD-level scientists in year-long federal or Congressional offices to inform US science and technology policy.
fellowships.notes: Flagship US science-policy fellowship; PhD required; 1-year federal/Congressional placement; post-defense target

fellowships.description: Fellowship supporting writers and researchers reporting on technology policy and its societal impact.
fellowships.terms: Researchers/policy professionals; part-time; remote; $10K stipend; 12 months
fellowships.notes: Independent tech-and-democracy reporting/analysis; values community-centered work; cohort opens ~fall; verify 2027 cycle. Source: techpolicy.press

fellowships.description: Award funding graduate students pursuing research-based degrees in science and engineering fields.
fellowships.terms: US citizen/PR; early-PhD stage; research-based STEM incl. SBE/education; $37K/yr x3 + tuition
fellowships.notes: Quantitative/data-methods framing fits; stage-limited (typically not >1 yr into PhD) — verify year-in-program eligibility. Source: NSF GRFP 25-547

fellowships.description: Two-year DC placement developing emerging leaders in technology and public policy.
fellowships.notes: 2-year DC placement; $75K junior / $113K full fellow. Look to apply for 2027 cycle

fellowships.description: Five-year award funding early-career faculty researchers studying youth-focused social policy.

fellowships.description: Fellowship supporting researchers addressing social justice and technology challenges.
fellowships.notes: Fits TF-IDF/Tableau work; tech & social justice framing. Look to apply for 2027 cycle

fellowships.description: Three-month in-person fellowship for scholars studying the societal and policy impacts of advanced AI.

fellowships.terms: Must have attended a prior ICPSR General Session
fellowships.notes: Open to students, faculty, and researchers from all disciplines who have previously attended a General Session of the ICPSR Summer Program.

fellowships.organization: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research (ICPSR)

fellowships.notes: Supports emerging scholars engaged in developing innovative empirical or methodological research in the social sciences. Open to incoming and currently enrolled graduate students at any US institution.

fellowships.description: Project grants funding applied initiatives that advance entrepreneurship and economic opportunity.

fellowships.description: Visiting research program hosting doctoral scholars working on AI and technology regulation in Europe.

fellowships.description: Internal Vanderbilt grant reimbursing graduate students up to $1,000 to present research at conferences.
fellowships.terms: 1 yr at Vanderbilt; first author/presenter; DGS approval; exhaust dept funding first
fellowships.notes: Up to $1,000 per trip. Must have completed one full year at Vanderbilt and be first author/presenter. Limit one grant per budget year (July 1–June 30); maximum three grants during your Vanderbilt tenure. Requires DGS approval and must exhaust departmental funding first.

fellowships.description: Vanderbilt Graduate Leadership Institute grants funding professional development and training activities.
fellowships.notes: Vanderbilt GLI Professional Development & Training Grants. See https://gradschool.vanderbilt.edu/student-resources/professional-development/gli/gli-funding-opportunities/

fellowships.description: Two-year federal fellowship placing early-career technologists on AI, cybersecurity, and data projects.

fellowships.notes: Requires U.S. work authorization; not employer-sponsored. Cohort typically starts August.

fellowships.description: Fellowship supporting emerging social entrepreneurs and leaders driving public-interest change.
fellowships.terms: Original founder; early-stage org (<5 yrs); any nationality; no degree req; $100K over 18 mo
fellowships.notes: Capacity-building + ~1,000-member network; fits founder track (Tarrant County Mediation Center, HRI). 2026 deadline Oct 8, 2 PM ET; notifications Jun/Jul. Source: echoinggreen.org

fellowships.description: Two-year program placing emerging leaders in full-time, paid state and local government roles.

fellowships.terms: Bachelor's required; visa sponsorship available; in-person at Princeton; appointment begins ~Sep 1
fellowships.notes: Bachelor's required; position is eligible for employment-related visa sponsorship.

fellowships.description: Grant supporting doctoral dissertation research in social policy.

fellowships.description: Fellowship placing mid-career professionals in policy or academic settings to bridge research and foreign affairs.

fellowships.description: Fellowship embedding mid-to-senior technology experts in federal agencies to drive AI and digital strategy.

fellowships.description: Remote part-time fellowship pairing students with state and local governments on tech-policy projects.
